name: gregium

metadata.name: gregium
metadata.summary: A simple package with easy features for using pygame

documentation: ![gregiumNameHD](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bf3c96d2-e1aa-4117-91cb-93d896145211)

classes.name: gregium.camera.Camera

methods.name: gregium.camera.Camera.zoom
documentation: Here we set zoom, clamp value to minimum of min_zoom and max of max_zoom.

methods.name: gregium.camera.Camera.position
documentation: Query the current offset.

methods.name: gregium.camera.Camera.position
documentation: Set the scroll offset directly.

methods.name: gregium.camera.Camera.move
documentation:
Move axis direction with scroll_speed.
Example: Move left -> move(-1, 0)
A simple 2D camera that contains the speed and offset.

classes.name: gregium.camera.CenteredCamera
documentation: A simple 2D camera class. 0, 0 will be the center of the screen, as opposed to the bottom left.

modules.name: gregium
documentation: Safe imports

modules.name: gregium.terminalLogging
documentation: An easy terminal logger capable of also saving to a file and timestamping

methods.name: gregium.terminalLogging.Logger.__init__
documentation:
Generates a basic terminal logger

Args:
    filepath:
        An optional filepath to save logs to
    enabled_print:
        Enables logger to log to terminal
    enabled_file:
        Enables logger to log to file (if specified)
    timestamp:
        Puts timestamps on each log

methods.name: gregium.terminalLogging.Logger.force_filepath_change
documentation: Will change the current filepath even if one is already loaded

methods.name: gregium.terminalLogging.Logger.base_log
documentation:
Logs type 'type' to terminal and file if allow_log is true

Args:
    message:
        The message to push
    source:
        A source for where the log is coming from
    flush:
        If sys.stdout should be flushed once message is given
    colorKey:
        ANSI color key

methods.name: gregium.terminalLogging.Logger.info
documentation:
Logs type 'INFO' to terminal and file if allow_log is true

Args:
    message:
        The message to push
    source:
        A source for where the log is coming from
    flush:
        If sys.stdout should be flushed once message is given

methods.name: gregium.terminalLogging.Logger.warn
documentation:
Logs type 'WARN' to terminal and file if allow_log is true

Args:
    message:
        The message to push
    source:
        A source for where the log is coming from
    flush:
        If sys.stdout should be flushed once message is given

methods.name: gregium.terminalLogging.Logger.error
documentation:
Logs type 'ERROR' to terminal and file if allow_log is true

Args:
    message:
        The message to push
    source:
        A source for where the log is coming from
    flush:
        If sys.stdout should be flushed once message is given

modules.name: gregium.env
documentation:
The original module for loading an saving .grg (ENV) files
Call gregenv.load() first and then env data is stored in gregenv.ENV as a dict

Revamp is pending

documentation: Loads ENV from .grg file using a path

functions.name: gregium.env.save
documentation: Saves ENV to file parent file

modules.name: gregium.old.gv
documentation:
A part of gregium meant only to store any globals that you need
if you have a global across files just use gregium.gv.<value name>

modules.name: gregium.buttons
documentation: Adds extra buttons not found in pyglet, such as push buttons from rects and text entry with borders

classes.name: gregium.buttons.PushButtonRect

methods.name: gregium.buttons.PushButtonRect.__init__
documentation:
Create a push button based on a rect.

Args:
    x:
        X coordinate of the push button.
    y:
        Y coordinate of the push button.
    pressed:
        Color to display when the button is pressed.
    depressed:
        Color to display when the button isn't pressed.
    hover:
        Color to display when the button is being hovered over.
    batch:
        Optional batch to add the push button to.
    label:
        Optional label to have on the text
    group:
        Optional parent group of the push button.

methods.name: gregium.buttons.PushButtonRect.on_press
documentation: Event: Dispatched when the button is clicked.

methods.name: gregium.buttons.PushButtonRect.on_release
documentation: Event: Dispatched when the button is released.
The base of all widgets.

classes.name: gregium.buttons.ToggleButtonRect

methods.name: gregium.buttons.ToggleButtonRect.on_toggle
documentation: Event: returns True or False to indicate the current state.

Instance of a toggle button.

Triggers the event 'on_toggle' when the mouse is pressed or released.

classes.name: gregium.buttons.SliderRect

methods.name: gregium.buttons.SliderRect.__init__
documentation:
Create a slider.

Args:
    x:
        X coordinate of the slider.
    y:
        Y coordinate of the slider.
    base:
        Color to display as the background to the slider.
    knob:
        Color of Knob that moves to show the position of the slider.
    edge:
        Pixels from the maximum and minimum position of the slider,
        to the edge of the base rect.
    batch:
        Optional batch to add the slider to.
    group:
        Optional parent group of the slider.

methods.name: gregium.buttons.SliderRect.on_change
documentation: Event: Returns the current value when the slider is changed.

Instance of a slider made of a base and a knob rect.

Triggers the event 'on_change' when the knob position is changed.
The knob position can be changed by dragging with the mouse, or
scrolling the mouse wheel.

classes.name: gregium.buttons.BorderedTextEntry

methods.name: gregium.buttons.BorderedTextEntry.__init__
documentation:
Create a text entry widget.

Args:
    text:
        Initial text to display.
    x:
        X coordinate of the text entry widget.
    y:
        Y coordinate of the text entry widget.
    width:
        The width of the text entry widget.
    color:
        The color of the outline box in RGBA format.
    text_color:
        The color of the text in RGBA format.
    caret_color:
        The color of the caret (when it is visible) in RGBA or RGB format.
    border:
        The width of the border
    border_color:
        The color of the border
    batch:
        Optional batch to add the text entry widget to.
    group:
        Optional parent group of text entry widget.

Instance of a text entry widget. Allows the user to enter and submit text.

Triggers the event 'on_commit', when the user hits the Enter or Return key.
The current text string is passed along with the event.

methods.name: gregium.buttons.MouseDistanceDetector.__init__
documentation:
Creates a widget that detects the mouse distance at any given position;
it is reccomended to not have this in a frame so it's able to detect distance no matter what

Args:
    text:
        Initial text to display.
    x:
        X coordinate of the text entry widget.
    y:
        Y coordinate of the text entry widget.

methods.name: gregium.buttons.MouseDistanceDetector.on_change
documentation: Event: Returns the mouse distance when it is moved.

modules.name: gregium.gamblecore
documentation: Gambling

methods.name: gregium.gamblecore.Gambler.__init__
documentation:
A gambler that can communicate with GameInst's to bet chips on it

Args:
    chips:
        Starting chips
    luck:
        Starting luck (0-100)

methods.name: gregium.gamblecore.GameBase.__init__
documentation: Base class for all gambling games

methods.name: gregium.gamblecore.GameBase.generate
documentation: Generates new game inst based on current game settings

methods.name: gregium.gamblecore.GameInst.__init__
documentation: Base class for allgambling game instances

methods.name: gregium.gamblecore.GameInst.play
documentation:
Runs a game based on inputs

Args:

        Inputs to the game, differs based on each game

methods.name: gregium.gamblecore.Casino.__init__
documentation:
A casino base capable of monitoring losses per table, dealing out chips, and more
Tables can be registered by doing .register(table)

Args:
    bank:
        The starting number of chips in the casino (-1 gives infinite)

methods.name: gregium.gamblecore.Casino.register
documentation:
Registers given table to casino for tracking,
will assign a unique id to the table

Args:
    table:
        The generated table class

methods.name: gregium.gamblecore.BlackjackInst.play
documentation:
Runs a game based on inputs

Args:
    inputs:
        Inputs to the game, MUST follow dictionary of
        "move":"hit"/"stand"

modules.name: gregium.old
documentation:
The core of Gregium

See documentation in README.md file

functions.name: gregium.old.alignPos
documentation:
Aligns a position to a corner of the window, possible corners to align to include, 
topRight, topLeft, bottomRight, bottomLeft, centerRight, 
centerLeft, centerTop, centerBottom, and center, 
each of which scale relative to the size of the window.
The default position is topLeft and running alignPos 
with topLeft returns the same value; 
bottomRight is the opposite corner and will add the 
total x & y values of the window respectively.

Will raise error if gregium.init() is not run first

functions.name: gregium.old.animRGB
documentation: Makes a list of all rgb values in order to transition from originalRGB to newRGB

functions.name: gregium.old.rotate
documentation:
Will rotate a point counterclockwise around a given origin, 
new point position is based on original distance to the origin, 
angle must be given in degree form for function to work properly.

*May have small rounding errors

functions.name: gregium.old.get_window_center
documentation:
Returns the center of the current working window.

*Will raise error if gregium.init() is not run first

functions.name: gregium.old.position_center
documentation:
Will return the coordinates required 
(assuming shape is blitted from top-left corner) 
in which the center of the object will be at original 
for given size.

*Not the same as get_center()

functions.name: gregium.old.get_center
documentation:
Will return the center of the shape assuming the original 
is in the top left of the given size.

*Not the same as position_center()

functions.name: gregium.old.get_rect_center
documentation:
Returns the center coordinates of the given pygame.Rect 
element based on the x & y coordinates of it.

functions.name: gregium.old.SpriteOnlyImg
documentation:
Generates a pygame.Surface without class information
First Surface is original image (for repeat changing)
Second Surface is modified image to current settings, 
if nothing is applied both surfaces will be the same
If image load fails, empty surface will be 
returned as well as having a warning

*For memory reasons, it is recommended to almost always set hasOneImage to True.
### ---- SPRITE HANDLER ---- ####

functions.name: gregium.old.on_press
documentation: Module only function, binds all keypresses and events to a respective value

functions.name: gregium.old.keyHandler
documentation: Module only function, catches all key press events to be processed by on_press funciton

functions.name: gregium.old.defaultButtonRender
documentation:
The default rendering for a gregium button, 
any alternatives must change BUTTONRENDERFUNC and have 1 
argument (name can be anything) and will pass in the button class
# -- DEFAULT BUTTON RENDER CODE -- ##

functions.name: gregium.old.cmdParseSeg
documentation: Function for parsing strings, integers, floats, and json

classes.name: gregium.old.FontType

methods.name: gregium.old.FontType.__init__
documentation:
Only used in Font.from_sys() and Font.from_file() so that 
extensions such as pylance or other python extensions that 
autocomplete will work correctly when initializing from the alternate methods.
### ---- FONT HANDLER ---- ####

methods.name: gregium.old.Font.blit
documentation:
Will blit text to the main working window at point pos unless altWindow is specified, 
all parameters are the same as normal pygame.freetype.Font.render() or 
pygame.freetype.Font.render_to() parameters; 
font will be fully left-aligned based on the pos parameter.

*Will raise an error if gregium.init() is not run first

methods.name: gregium.old.Font.blit_center
documentation:
Will blit text to the main working window with center 
located at point pos unless altWindow is specified, 
all parameters are the same as normal pygame.freetype.Font.render() or 
pygame.freetype.Font.render_to() parameters; 
font will be fully left-aligned based on the pos parameter.

*Will raise an error if gregium.init() is not run first

methods.name: gregium.old.Font.blit_true_center
documentation:
Will blit text to the main working window with 
center located at point pos unless altWindow is specified, 
all parameters are the same as normal pygame.freetype.Font.render() or 
pygame.freetype.Font.render_to() parameters; 
font will be fully center-aligned based on the pos parameter.

*Will raise an error if gregium.init() is not run first

methods.name: gregium.old.Sprite.updateImage
documentation:
Updates the image for animation/movement


* The order should always be 
#1 updateImage() 
#2 updateDropShadow() only if applicable
#3 tint_add/tint_mult
#4 blit/blit_center/blit_pivot_center
#5 testColl/testCollR unless you define the "pos" argument

methods.name: gregium.old.Sprite.updateDropShadow
documentation:
Generates a drop shadow for the current frame of the sprite

*Required for any drop shadow to render at all and run every frame of drop shadow before blit

methods.name: gregium.old.Sprite.tint_add
documentation: Tint the sprite with the given color

methods.name: gregium.old.Sprite.tint_mult
documentation: Multiplies each pixel on sprite by rgb tint

methods.name: gregium.old.Sprite.blit
documentation:
Blits the targeted sprite onto the given surface. 
The top left of the sprite will be positioned at the provided coordinate 
pair PLUS the current SCRLX and SCRLY (x + SCRLX, y + SCRLY).
Dropshadow argument controls the x and y offset of the dropshadow (0,0 does not render)

methods.name: gregium.old.Sprite.blit_center
documentation:
Blits the targeted sprite onto the given surface. 
The center of the sprite will be positioned at the provided 
coordinate pair PLUS the current SCRLX and SCRLY (x + SCRLX, y + SCRLY).
Dropshadow argument controls the x and y offset of the dropshadow (0,0 does not render)

methods.name: gregium.old.Sprite.blit_pivot_center
documentation:
Blits the targeted sprite onto the given surface. 
The center of the sprite will be positioned at the provided coordinate 
pair PLUS the current SCRLX and SCRLY (x + SCRLX, y + SCRLY). The sprite 
will be rotated around the coordinate point of the “pivot” argument by 
COUNTERCLOCKWISE by the number of degrees represented by the “angle” argument.
Dropshadow argument controls the x and y offset of the dropshadow (0,0 does not render)

methods.name: gregium.old.Sprite.blitFixed
documentation:
Blits the targeted sprite onto the given surface. 
The sprite will be positioned at its same previous coordinate 
pair PLUS the current SCRLX and SCRLY (x + SCRLX, y + SCRLY).
The dropshadow on this blit is instead a relative position to where it last was

methods.name: gregium.old.Sprite.testColl
documentation:
Tests to see if the sprite collides with another sprite 
(must be gregium.sprite type),
if either pos argument is not supplied it will use the most 
recent position blitted by the sprite as the position 
(scroll is taken into account)

methods.name: gregium.old.Sprite.testCollR
documentation:
Tests to see if the sprite collides with any other rects 
(must be pygame.Rect type)
if the pos argument is not supplied it will use the most 
recent position blitted by the sprite as the position 
(scroll is taken into account)

methods.name: gregium.old.Sprite.updateSheet
documentation:
Updates the active sprite in the spritesheet. 
This function should be used in the game loop and, 
in most cases, should be updated every frame. 
By changing the “sheetAnimMS” value it will change 
how long (in ms) it takes for each frame of the sprite to update

methods.name: gregium.old.Sprite.scale
documentation:
Scales the sprite by scale argument factor, 
there should be only 1 input unless you 
are changing both width and height 
(don't do scale & width or scale & height it will not work correctly)
If either width or height is blank it is assumed to use automatic
and will scale based on the other changed value

methods.name: gregium.old.ziphandle.zipFolder
documentation: Zips a folder without recursion

methods.name: gregium.old.EV_Initializer.__init__
documentation:
Used only in gregium to set up events, there is no reason to 
initialize this class outside of its initialization in gregium

methods.name: gregium.old.EV_Initializer.clearEvent
documentation: Resets all events to default values (use before event loop)

methods.name: gregium.old.EV_Initializer.supplyEvent
documentation:
Gives pygame events to gregium 
(events supplied must be from pygame.event.get()
from each for iteration, to put it simply use 
<for event in pygame.event.get()> 
and use this function with event as param)

classes.name: gregium.old.button

methods.name: gregium.old.button.__init__
documentation:
Generates a simple button. The following arguments can be provided to customize the button:
pos: a tuple that determines the (x, y) coordinates of where to place the top left corner of the button.
size: a tuple that determines the width and height of the button, respectively.
color: an RGB tuple that fills the button with the given RGB color. Defaults to (255, 255, 255), which is white.
outline: an RGB tuple that fills the button's border with the given RGB color. Has no effect if outlineThick is zero, i.e. there is no border.
outlineThick: an integer that determines the thickness of the button's border in pixels. Defaults to five (5) pixels.
suppliedFont: a Font object that should be set up with gregium's Font instantiator. Defaults to None, which will load the SpaceMono font.
text: a string that represents text inside the button. By default, there is no text.
textCol: an RGB tuple that specifies what color the text should be. Defaults to (0, 0, 0), which is black.
textSize: an integer that represents the size of the text in standard text size (1/72th of an inch equals 1 text size point).
colorHighlight: an RGB tuple that fills in the background of only the text with the provided RGB color. Defaults to (200, 200, 200), which is gray.
outlineHighlight: an RGB tuple that fills the outline of the text with the given RGB color. Defaults to (55, 55, 55), which is dark gray.
align: a string that specifies how the text should be aligned within the button.
rounding: follows general rules of pygame rect rounding, higher values yield more rounding
### ---- BUTTON HANDLER ---- ####

methods.name: gregium.old.textBox.__init__
documentation: Documentation comes later, bad code comes first :thumbsup:

methods.name: gregium.old.alertBox.__init__
documentation:
suppliedFont: a gregium Font object that alters the font of alert text.
buttons: a tuple with as many strings as desired, strings will be converted to buttons and spread horizontally across the box, if the amount of buttons is only 1, use ('buttonText',) as opposed to ('buttonText') to prevent errors in generation. These buttons will be displayed on the alert as options to click on. An example of buttons that can be added are “Accept” or “Deny” buttons.
title: a string containing the title that will be displayed on the alert. The alert's title can be multi-line if \n is put in the string. Defaults to None.
color: an RGB tuple that will fill the background color of the entire alert box with that RGB color. Defaults to (0, 0, 0), which is white.
outline: an RGB tuple of the outline of the alert box. Defaults to (255, 255, 255), which is black.
textCol: an RGB tuple that will change the text color using that RGB color. Defaults to (255, 255, 255), which is black.

methods.name: gregium.old.alertBox.render
documentation: Render an alert. Will return 0 on no buttons pressed. Returns the pressed button if one is clicked

methods.name: gregium.old.CLI.addCmd
documentation:
add a new command! Syntax is as follows
{"name":{"root":{"type":"*","var":"test","next":"foo"},"foo":{"type":"*","var":"test2","next":"etc"}}}

*Types include, str, json, int, float, literal, func
int & float can have min and max
literal must have a list of outputs
func must have a "run" variable instead of next and var and the "run" variable must have the function in it
you can input multiple commands by having multiple in the furthest outside dict
repeat commands will not get overwritten but will instead throw an error

methods.name: gregium.old.CLI.helpcmd
documentation: Generate a help message for using commands

methods.name: gregium.old.CLI.run
documentation: Read a full command from a string and output code (error, return) or (0, return) on success
